defs: Having qualities that may infect; communicable or caused by infection; pestilential; epidemic; as, an infectious fever; infectious clothing; infectious water; infectious vices.
Corrupting, or tending to corrupt or contaminate; vitiating; demoralizing.
quotes: It [the court] is necessary for the polishing of manners . . . but it is infectious even to the best morals to live always in it.

defs: Contaminating with illegality; exposing to seizure and forfeiture.
quotes: Contraband articles are said to be of an infectious nature.

defs: Capable of being easily diffused or spread; sympathetic; readily communicated; as, infectious mirth.
quotes: The laughter was so genuine as to be infectious.
